record.field: Huly is an all-in-one, open-source team workspace that unifies project management, knowledge, chat and CRM in a single application.

Huly is an all-in-one, open-source team workspace that unifies project management, knowledge, chat and CRM in a single application.

Built as a self-hosted alternative to tools like Linear, Jira, Slack, Notion and Motion, Huly bundles issue tracking, documents, real-time messaging and business apps so teams can work without constant context switching. It runs as a Docker-based microservices stack and is aimed at engineering and product teams that want to own their data instead of relying on multiple SaaS subscriptions.

Key features
  • Project and issue tracking with sprints, roadmaps and customizable workflows
  • Collaborative documents and a structured knowledge base
  • Built-in team chat and real-time notifications
  • Additional business modules: CRM, HRM and applicant tracking (ATS)
  • Bi-directional GitHub synchronization and third-party integrations
  • Self-hosted deployment via Docker Compose with unlimited users
Best for

Engineering, product and startup teams looking to consolidate project tracking, documentation and communication into one self-hosted platform while keeping full control of their data.

field.name: tag_ids
field.eval: [Command.set([ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_self_hosted'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_project_management'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_collaboration'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_productivity'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_documentation'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_notes'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_chat'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_crm'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_communication')])]

record.field: #!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose exec -T mongodb sh -c 'mongodump --archive' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.archive" echo "mongodump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.archive") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d mongodb sleep 8 docker compose exec -T mongodb sh -c 'mongorestore --drop --archive' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.archive" echo "mongorestore OK"
